raw_input:
[5.0/5] Very pleased with this game! I just opened up my copy of this game today and gave it a shot after reading over the rules a few times. Although I played solo (which is not suggested in any way since you control at least 6 factions yourself), I found the game to be very enjoyable. I could see where this could easily become a favorite among friends, whether or not we played as teams. Here are a few things to note:
+The box that the game comes in is very well made (Looks great too)
+Great quality for the game board (It is a 6-fold board, by the way)
+Cards are very good quality
+Tons of cubes (90/color; 720 total)
+Excellent mechanics for determining turns for players (In my opinion)
+Multiple tie-breakers
+Low required playtime with regards to it being a strategy game (especially compared to Risk)
+/- Limited Rounds are implemented instead of any particular "end goals" (I personally think this is genius; some people may not prefer it)

Edit:
This game is a hit amongst friends. It's relatively simple to teach (as long as people are paying attention) and flows pretty smoothly. Almost anytime my wife and I go to an event where board games are present this one has been requested almost every time. [5.0/5] Great game This game is really fun it is a based loosely on Risk but much better rules and gets rid of the worst of Risk. The game play is fairly quick (90 minutes which is good for a strategy game) as there is a dice that counts down unlike in Risk which goes on forever. You really have to be strategic in your turns to win. You can play multiple people which is the most fun or multiple factions with less people still fun but I find that people tend to focus on one and sacrifice one so its a bit less fun. Great game overall. I highly recommend [4.0/5] Even though I don't normally like games like Risk... I purchased this game through the Kickstarter campaign. I'll admit, I am friends with the creator but I wouldn't give this game a good rating if I didn't enjoy it. I play lots of table top games (Pandemic, Ticket to Ride, Dominion) and am a big fan of Wil Wheaton's show TableTop on Youtube, so I would have tried Spheres of Influence even if I didn't know the creator. I was worried that I wouldn't like it since I normally don't care for global domination-type games (Risk, Axis & Allies) I find them too long and more frustrating than fun. However, this game was fast paced, and very engaging. There was significant strategy involved, and there are added elements (regional resources, oil production, and capital cities) that make the strategy a lot more interesting than just battling your opponents. I played with 5 people--a mix of people who like global domination games and a couple who don't. Everyone really enjoyed it. Took about an hour to complete. Good quality game components. [5.0/5] Got this game on the basis of a DiceTower review ... Got this game on the basis of a DiceTower review for it. I agree with the review in that this is a "Risk killer," i.e., probably would never want to play standard Risk given having this game available. With the variable starting positions, resource concerns, and action card play, this game goes well beyond the move and roll pattern that characterizes standard Risk. [5.0/5] If you love the original style RISK, toss it and buy this game. Like other reviewers here, I backed this game on Kickstarter. As someone else said, it was a very well run campaign. One of the best I've seen. It's a good game, good quality components, has a good price, and it shipped on time (which is a rarity on Kickstarter).

As to why I backed the game and why I love it? Again, as others have said, this is a "RISK killer" game. When I find a game that does the same thing as another game, but better, the old game goes (donated typically). This game is obviously like RISK, however it is different in some important ways.

1. Random Starting Locations: You're dealt two location cards and pick one.
2. You don't fill the board, you only start in your beginning location, so much the board is empty and you must rush to/fight for them.
3. One move at a time. You move from one 'territory' to another one territory at a time. No insanely long turns where someone invades multiple other territories at a time.
4. There are turns and there are rounds. Each player takes multiple turns within each round and one of the very cool things is those turns are randomized, so you don't know when during a round you're going to get to take your turn, which adds a lot to the strategy aspect.
5. You do get cards like in RISK, but they are more meaningful and provide far better options than merely more armies.
6. Water movement exists and how troops move in and battle in or from the water is a good balance of strategic and realistic vs simple.
7. These days it seems hard to find a game that allows for more then four or five players. This allows for up to eight
8. The game is very simple, but very strategic. You can learn to play it in a few minutes, but the subtleties of strategy will likely take some time to see, which allows more people to enjoy the game and helps with it's longevity.
9. Lastly the game has a time limit. After so many rounds you see who is ahead. So no 12 hours marathon games like traditional RISK. [5.0/5] Like risk but better. Well made game Great game. Makes you plan out your moves better than stacking up troops in risk and conquering the world. Plus turn limit helps with the time of the game. Last more than 2 hours depending on the number of players. Very unique artwork. Battle system is cool. Makes it more fair for the defender without making the attacker too overpowered when they have a lot of troops. [5.0/5] My Favorite Game This is probably my favorite board game ever. Since we got it last summer, we've played about 30 times. We loop in new people all the time and everyone loves it. It's a well thought out, dynamic, better version of Risk. Highly recommended. [5.0/5] This game takes everything annoying about Risk This game takes everything annoying about Risk, and makes it way better. There's no reason, beyond nostalgia, to play Risk again. The turn system is great, the combat is better. The objectives like points of interest(earn special ability cards) and oil fields(earn more turns per round) make the game much more interesting than just holding choke points. While I personally like long-game world domination strategy, the round limit is pretty refreshing, and gives you a little more excitement and sense of urgency. Obviously, you could just remove the turn limit if you wanted to, but I'd recommend trying it first for sure. The rules are fairly simple and make a lot of sense. Once you get going it's a breeze, allowing strategy to be your primary concern.

This is absolutely worth buying. The Dice Tower on Youtube(not affiliated, just a fan) has a nice review if you want a better idea, or the creator(Josh Lamont) has a longer video that teaches you step by step how to play. [5.0/5] Great game that rivals the likes of Risk I’ve been on the fence about purchasing this game but decided to pull the trigger and try it out.

The game itself is a great strategy game that has a lot more action compared to a standard Risk game. It starts off a bit slow but when the last two to three rounds come, the game ramps up and factions go up and down the score tracker with every turn. The only qualm I have is the tracker board is a bit flimsy. [5.0/5] Lovely game. I've played 4 times so far and ... Lovely game. I've played 4 times so far and there is still so much to learn. Everyone I have played with enjoys it as well. [5.0/5] Risk with no headache! I really like the combat mechanic in this game. It prevents someone from just sweeping the board. It's also interesting to see how empires spread across the globe and how the game will mimic history. This is easily one of, if not my favorite new game. [5.0/5] This game is deceptively simple in its easy play; however I have been playing war games and simulations for over 45 years. I have collected over 300+ war games and have played them all with both friends old and new. This game is deceptively simple in its easy play; however, it requires sound strategy to win. The rules are easy to learn, but that's when the fun starts as there are so many possible strategies to choose from.

The game components and board are high quality and nicely done. This demonstrates real commitment by the designer, to hold out for the best.

I will need more time to thoroughly evaluate this game, but I believe that out of the 300+, it will land firmly in my top ten. [5.0/5] Five Stars This game is really fun! The artwork of the board/cards looks great. [5.0/5] Awesome Game! One of my favorite board games of all time! If you like Risk, you'll LOVE playing this game. Spheres has a lot of options in the game play which makes far more interesting and fun to play. It is fast paced, alway changing and plays typically in about 90 minutes. After you play once, you're ready to play straight away again! One other great thing, unlike Risk, you'll never be left out of the game. My friends and I usually play it several times in a row. If you like playing world domination games, Spheres of Influence is the right game for you! [5.0/5] Great game! Very entertaining Great game! Very entertaining, can be played equitably with 2 people.The board it's self is nice and thick with great coloring. The score card could be a little bigger and made of nicer paper. [5.0/5] One of my top five games easily. Super deep strategy One of my top five games easily. Super deep strategy, quick pacing, and best of all ***it ends!!!**** it does not have monopoly or risk syndrome where you can only win by defeating everyone else. I kickstarter the game and have had it for some months, playing it several times [5.0/5] Spheres Influenced Me! The componant quality is top notch and the board is gorgeous! The easiest way to describe it would be to say it plays somewhat like Risk, though it has much more interesting mechanics and depth of gameplay. [5.0/5] Love the randomized turn deck mechanic and its so great ... One of my gaming group's favorites! Love the randomized turn deck mechanic and its so great that non-Risk people will sit down, play, and enjoy.

Also - all the backstabbing you want but very few broken hearts!

response:
Game good. Risk but better. Many people say same thing - Risk killer. Game end when rounds finish, no marathon play like Risk. Take about 90 minutes most time.

Components strong. Board thick, good quality. Cards good. Tons of cubes - 720 total. Score tracker maybe little flimsy.

Mechanics interesting. Turn order random - not know when get turn in round. Move one territory at time, no big sweeping conquests. Cards give real options, not just more armies. Special things on map - oil fields give more turns, capital cities give special cards. Water movement exist too. Up to 8 players possible.

Rules easy learn but strategy deep. New players pick up fast. Game still interesting after many plays. One person play 30 times since summer. Another person, 45 years war game experience, say land in top 10 of 300+ games.

Friends enjoy. Non-strategy people sit down and like it. Good for groups mix of gamers and non-gamers. Play multiple times in row because fun and quick.
